ud8.10:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud8.10:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
ud8.10:2.1:
“When Dabba Mallaputta rose up into the air and, sitting cross-legged in the sky, in space,
entered & emerged from the fire property and was totally unbound,
his body burned and was consumed so that neither ashes nor soot could be discerned.
ud8.10:2.2: Just as when ghee or oil is burned and consumed, neither ashes nor soot can be discerned,
ud8.10:2.3:
in the same way, when Dabba Mallaputta rose up into the air and, sitting cross-legged in the sky, in space,
entered & emerged from the fire property and was totally unbound,
his body burned and was consumed so that neither ashes nor soot could be discerned.”
ud8.10:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.10:4.1: Just as the destination of a glowing fire
ud8.10:4.4:
isn’t known:
Even so, there’s no destination to describe
ud8.10:5.2: –having crossed over the flood of sensuality’s bond–
ud8.10:5.4: for those who’ve attained unwavering bliss.
